text: If you put your effort and concentration into playing to your potential, to be the best that you can be, I don't care what the scoreboard says at the end of the game, in my book we're gonna be winners.
context: The defining philosophy: effort and potential matter more than the final score or the size of the opponent.

text: My practices aren't designed for your enjoyment.
context: Setting the tone that hard work and discipline come before fun or comfort.

text: Five players on the floor functioning as one single unit: team, team, team – no one more important than the other.
context: Emphasizing that the team is greater than any individual star.

text: I've seen you guys can shoot but there's more to the game than shooting. There's fundamentals and defense.
context: Teaching that flashy skills mean nothing without the boring, essential basics.

text: There's a tradition in tournament play to not talk about the next step until you've climbed the one in front of you.
context: Focus only on the immediate task; don't get ahead of yourself with dreams of glory.

text: Forget about the crowds, the size of the school, their fancy uniforms, and remember what got you here. Focus on the fundamentals.
context: Ignore intimidation and external noise; return to the basics that earned the opportunity.

text: You are in the Army. You're in my Army. Every day between three and five.
context: Establishing total commitment and structure for the season.

text: I would hope you would support who we are. Not, who we are not.
context: A plea for the town to embrace the team's identity and style rather than comparing to others.
